title: Zhaorun Chen \(^{1*}\) , Zhen Xiang \(^{2}\) , Chaowei Xiao \(^{3}\) , Dawn Song \(^{4}\) , Bo Li \(^{1,2*}\) \(^{1}\) University of Chicago, \(^{2}\) University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign \(^{3}\) University of Wisconsin, Madison \(^{4}\) University of California, Berkeley
contributions_summary: AGENTPOISON introduces the first backdoor attack targeting RAG-based LLM agents by poisoning their memory or knowledge base with minimal malicious instances (<0.1% poison rate), using a novel constrained optimization framework to generate stealthy, coherent triggers that map queries into a compact, unique region of the embedding space—ensuring high retrieval probability of poisoned demonstrations while preserving benign performance (≤1% degradation). Unlike prior attacks, AGENTPOISON requires no model retraining, achieves ≥80% attack success rate across three real-world agents (autonomous driving, QA, healthcare), and exhibits strong transferability across diverse RAG embedders, including black-box models like OpenAI ADA, while evading defenses based on perplexity filtering and rephrasing through semantic coherence.
